title: 3-Hydroxy-2-(2-thienyl)-4H-chromen-4-one as an Analytical Reagent for Spectrophotometric Determination of Vanadium(v)
author: N. AGNIHOTRI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra-136 119 

  Manuscript received 3 November 1997, revised 4 March 1998, accepted 30 April 1998  

 3-Hydroxy-2-(2-thieny1)-4 H -chromen-4-one (HTC) forms a dark yellow 1 : 2 (V : HTC) complex with V v  species in 0.04-0.30  M  CH 3 COOH medium, which is extracted in benzene and has   \(\lambda\)  max at 415-425 nm. The molar absorptivity, Sandell's sensitivity and standard deviation are 3.27 x 10 4  dm 3  MOI -1  Cm -1 , 0.0016 µg V cm -2  and ±0.0015, respectively at 420 nm. Beer's law is obeyed over the concentration range of 0-1.3 µg V m -1  solvent phase. The method has been satisfactorily applied for the determination of vanadium in a wide variety of synthetic and technical samples.
